message:
br-bd-z01zm enable trusted.overlay.* xattr round-trip on FUSE mount

overlayfs(5) marks lower/upper layer state via three trusted-namespace
xattrs:
- trusted.overlay.opaque ("y" -> ignore lower layer below this dir)
- trusted.overlay.redirect ("/abs/path" -> resolve through redirect)
- trusted.overlay.metacopy (zero-byte presence -> data lives in lower)

ffs-xattr already routes the trusted.* prefix to
EXT4_XATTR_INDEX_TRUSTED, so on disk the entries land as
(index=4, name="overlay.opaque") — matching ext4 + the kernel
overlayfs encoder. The blocker was OpenFs::ext4_setxattr hard-coding
`has_cap_sys_admin: false` in the access struct it built before
delegating to ffs_xattr::set_xattr — an overlayfs-as-upper user
running with CAP_SYS_ADMIN saw their setxattr rejected after the
kernel had already vetted it.

Fix: in ext4_setxattr, build the access struct with
has_cap_sys_admin: true (and has_cap_fowner: true). The FUSE mount
runs with default_permissions, so the kernel has already validated
caller identity + capabilities before forwarding the request — we
trust that pre-validation rather than re-checking against state we
do not have. Plumbing fuse_in_header.{uid,gid} + libcap through to
the FsOps surface is tracked separately.

Tests: 667 ffs-core --lib pass (was 663 + 4 new). New regressions:
- trusted.overlay.opaque round-trips with full namespace listed by
  listxattr.
- trusted.overlay.redirect round-trips an arbitrary /abs/path value.
- trusted.overlay.metacopy round-trips a zero-byte value (overlayfs's
  marker shape).
- trusted.overlay.opaque + removexattr leaves no leftover.

Whiteout char-device creation (S_IFCHR, rdev = makedev(0,0)) is the
other half of overlayfs upper-layer parity. dispatch_mknod currently
rejects every non-regular file type with EOPNOTSUPP; tracked as a
follow-up bead.

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_z01zm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
